skills.name: Text Display
skills.desc: Text display tasks cover various ways to display text on the terminal, making it readable and presentable.

skills.name: Screen Clearing
skills.desc: Screen clearing involves removing or clearing the terminal screen, providing a clean slate for new content or interactions.

skills.name: Arithmetic Calculations
skills.desc: Arithmetic calculations involve performing mathematical operations in Linux, such as addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.

skills.name: Logic Operations
skills.desc: Logic operations encompass tasks related to logical operations and expressions, enabling decision-making and control flow in scripts and commands.

skills.name: Text Columnizing
skills.desc: Text columnizing helps format and align text data in columns for better readability.

skills.name: Condition Testing
skills.desc: Condition testing involves evaluating logical expressions to make decisions in scripts and commands.

skills.name: Command Building
skills.desc: Command building refers to constructing complex commands by combining multiple commands and options.

skills.name: Directory Tree Display
skills.desc: Displaying directory trees visually represents the hierarchical structure of directories and subdirectories.

skills.name: Command Assistance
skills.desc: Command assistance provides help and information about commands and their usage.

skills.name: Manual Access
skills.desc: Accessing manual pages (man pages) provides detailed documentation and information about commands.

skills.name: Line Numbering
skills.desc: Line numbering adds line numbers to text files for reference and identification.

skills.name: Input Reading
skills.desc: Input reading allows scripts and commands to read user input for interactive behavior.

skills.name: Text Formatting
skills.desc: Text formatting involves applying formatting styles to text for presentation purposes.

skills.name: Execution Delaying
skills.desc: Delaying execution is used for scheduling tasks or creating time intervals in scripts.

skills.name: Variable Declaring
skills.desc: Declaring variables is fundamental for storing and manipulating data in scripts.

skills.name: Script Executing
skills.desc: Executing scripts runs custom sequences of commands, automating tasks and processes.

skills.name: Shell Exiting
skills.desc: Exiting the shell terminates the current shell session or script execution.

skills.name: Content Listing
skills.desc: Content listing involves listing the contents of directories and files, allowing users to view what is present in a specified location.

skills.name: File Creating/Updating
skills.desc: File creation and updating involve creating new files or modifying existing ones. This is essential for managing data and text files in Linux.

skills.name: File Copying
skills.desc: Copying files allows you to duplicate files or directories, making backups or replicating data when needed.

skills.name: File Moving/Renaming
skills.desc: Moving or renaming files involves changing their location or changing their names, providing better organization and management.

skills.name: File Removing
skills.desc: Removing files is used to delete unwanted data from the file system, freeing up storage space.

skills.name: Link Creating
skills.desc: Creating symbolic or hard links allows for efficient file access and management by creating references to files.

skills.name: File Concatenating
skills.desc: Concatenating files involves combining the contents of multiple files into a single file, often used for data analysis or text processing.

skills.name: File Beginning Display
skills.desc: Displaying the beginning of a file is useful for viewing the initial contents of large files without loading the entire file into memory.

skills.name: File End Display
skills.desc: Displaying the end of a file helps examine the concluding portions of large files efficiently.

skills.name: Text Counting
skills.desc: Counting text occurrences is crucial for analyzing data and generating statistics from text files.

skills.name: Text Cutting
skills.desc: Text cutting allows you to extract specific portions of text from files for further processing.

skills.name: File Paging
skills.desc: Paging through files enables users to read large files step by step for better comprehension and navigation.

skills.name: File Scrolling
skills.desc: Scrolling through files provides a dynamic way to view file contents, particularly useful for log files and long documents.

skills.name: Ownership Changing
skills.desc: Changing file ownership is essential for managing access control and permissions in a Linux system.

skills.name: Permission Modifying
skills.desc: Modifying file permissions allows you to control who can read, write, and execute files, enhancing security and access control.

skills.name: Directory Changing
skills.desc: Changing directories is the basic operation for navigating the file system in Linux.

skills.name: Directory Displaying
skills.desc: Displaying directory contents is essential for exploring the contents of folders.

skills.name: Directory Creating
skills.desc: Creating directories is necessary for organizing files and data into a structured hierarchy.

skills.name: Wildcard Character
skills.desc: Using wildcard characters like '*' and '?' in file operations allows for flexible and powerful file matching and manipulation.

skills.name: File Searching
skills.desc: Searching for files helps locate specific files or patterns within a directory or across the entire system.

skills.name: File Locating
skills.desc: Locating files provides a way to find the path of an executable file in the system.

skills.name: Command Locating
skills.desc: Locating commands helps users identify the location of executable commands in the system.

skills.name: File/Command Finding
skills.desc: Finding files or commands combines search functionality with useful information about file or command locations.

skills.name: Pattern Searching
skills.desc: Pattern searching is crucial for text processing, allowing you to find specific text patterns within files.

skills.name: Stream Editing
skills.desc: Stream editing involves making changes to text data as it is being read or written, typically used in text processing pipelines.

skills.name: Text Processing
skills.desc: Text processing involves manipulating and processing text data using various commands and techniques.

skills.name: Text Sorting
skills.desc: Sorting text allows for organizing data alphabetically or numerically, making it easier to analyze and work with.

skills.name: Duplicate Filtering
skills.desc: Filtering duplicates is useful for removing redundant data and ensuring data quality.

skills.name: Character Translating
skills.desc: Character translation helps replace specific characters or character sets in text data.

skills.name: Line Feed Filtering
skills.desc: Line feed filtering is used to process and clean up text data by removing or replacing line breaks.

skills.name: Line Merging
skills.desc: Merging lines combines multiple lines of text into one, facilitating data consolidation.

skills.name: File Joining
skills.desc: Joining files combines the contents of multiple files into a single file, useful for data integration.

skills.name: Evaluate Expressions
skills.desc: The expr command evaluates basic arithmetic expressions in the Linux command line, with mandatory spaces between operators and operands.

skills.name: Output Multiplexing
skills.desc: Output multiplexing allows you to send output to multiple destinations, such as files and the screen, simultaneously.

skills.name: Data Piping
skills.desc: Data piping involves sending the output of one command as input to another, creating powerful data processing pipelines.

skills.name: I/O Redirecting
skills.desc: I/O redirecting allows you to control the input and output of commands, enabling automation and customization.

skills.name: Job Managing
skills.desc: Job managing involves controlling and monitoring background jobs and processes on the system.

skills.name: Background Running
skills.desc: Background running refers to executing commands or processes in the background, allowing continued use of the terminal.

skills.name: Job Foregrounding
skills.desc: Job foregrounding brings background jobs to the foreground, allowing interaction with them.

skills.name: Process Terminating
skills.desc: Process terminating is used to stop or end running processes or jobs.

skills.name: Multi-Process Killing
skills.desc: Multi-process killing enables users to terminate multiple processes simultaneously.

skills.name: Pattern-Based Killing
skills.desc: Pattern-based killing allows for terminating processes that match a specific pattern or criteria.

skills.name: Process Waiting
skills.desc: Process waiting involves waiting for a specific process or job to complete before proceeding.

skills.name: Background Management
skills.desc: Background management covers the control and monitoring of background processes, jobs, and tasks.

skills.name: Disk Space Reporting
skills.desc: Disk space reporting provides information about disk usage, helping users manage storage resources.

skills.name: File Space Estimating
skills.desc: File space estimating calculates the space required for files and directories, assisting in storage planning.

skills.name: File System Mounting
skills.desc: File system mounting allows users to attach external storage devices or network shares to the file system.

skills.name: Command Repeating
skills.desc: Command repeating is used to execute commands repeatedly, often with specified intervals.

skills.name: Job Scheduling
skills.desc: Job scheduling involves setting up automated tasks to run at specific times or intervals using tools like cron.

skills.name: System Information Displaying
skills.desc: System information displaying provides details about the system's hardware, software, and configuration.

skills.name: Hostname Managing
skills.desc: Hostname managing includes tasks related to configuring and managing the system's hostname.

skills.name: Process Displaying
skills.desc: Process displaying lists the currently running processes on the system, including their status and resource usage.

skills.name: Task Displaying
skills.desc: Task displaying provides information about background tasks, jobs, and their status.

skills.name: Memory Reporting
skills.desc: Memory reporting displays information about system memory usage, including RAM and swap space.

skills.name: Date/Time Displaying
skills.desc: Date/time displaying shows the current date and time, which is often used for log entries and scheduling.

skills.name: Command Timing
skills.desc: Command timing measures the execution time of commands or scripts, useful for performance analysis.

skills.name: File Converting/Copying
skills.desc: File converting/copying involves converting file formats or copying data between different formats or locations.

skills.name: Service Managing
skills.desc: Service managing covers the control and management of system services and daemons.

skills.name: Archiving
skills.desc: Archiving involves creating compressed archive files to store multiple files or directories in a single package.

skills.name: Compressing
skills.desc: Compressing files reduces their size, saving storage space and speeding up data transfer.

skills.name: Gzip
skills.desc: gzip is a file format and a software application used for file compression and decompression.

skills.name: Decompressing
skills.desc: Decompressing files is the process of restoring compressed files to their original state.

skills.name: User Adding
skills.desc: User adding involves creating new user accounts in the system.

skills.name: User Removing
skills.desc: User removing is the process of deleting user accounts from the system.

skills.name: User Modifying
skills.desc: User modifying includes tasks like changing user attributes, such as username, password, and home directory.

skills.name: Password Changing
skills.desc: Password changing allows users to update their passwords for security purposes.

skills.name: Privilege Granting
skills.desc: Privilege granting involves assigning additional permissions or privileges to users or groups.

skills.name: User Switching
skills.desc: User switching allows users to change their active user context to access different privileges or environments.

skills.name: Group Displaying
skills.desc: Group displaying lists the existing user groups in the system.

skills.name: Group Adding
skills.desc: Group adding involves creating new user groups in the system.

skills.name: Group Removing
skills.desc: Group removing is the process of deleting user groups from the system.

skills.name: Group Changing
skills.desc: Group changing includes tasks like modifying group attributes or membership.

skills.name: User Identifying
skills.desc: User identifying provides information about the current user's identity and permissions.

skills.name: Environment Managing
skills.desc: Environment managing involves configuring user environment variables and settings.

skills.name: User/Group ID Displaying
skills.desc: Displaying user and group IDs provides numeric identifiers for users and groups.

skills.name: Shell Setting
skills.desc: Shell setting allows users to specify their preferred shell for command-line interactions.

skills.name: Variable Exporting
skills.desc: Variable exporting makes environment variables available to child processes and scripts.

skills.name: Variable Unsetting
skills.desc: Variable unsetting removes environment variables from the current shell session.

skills.name: Secure Connecting
skills.desc: Secure connecting involves establishing secure connections to remote servers or systems, often using SSH.

skills.name: Network Connecting
skills.desc: Network connecting includes tasks like connecting to remote servers, managing network connections, and troubleshooting network issues.

skills.name: Secure Copying
skills.desc: Secure copying allows for securely transferring files between local and remote systems using protocols like SCP.

skills.name: Secure File Transferring
skills.desc: Secure file transferring involves encrypting and transferring files securely over a network.

skills.name: File Transferring
skills.desc: File transferring includes methods for transferring files between local and remote systems over a network.

skills.name: Networking Utility
skills.desc: Networking utility tools provide various functionalities for network management and diagnostics.

skills.name: Network Configuring
skills.desc: Network configuring covers the setup and configuration of network interfaces and parameters.

skills.name: Network Monitoring
skills.desc: Network monitoring involves monitoring network performance, usage, and activity.

skills.name: Network Testing
skills.desc: Network testing is used to assess network connectivity and diagnose network-related issues.

skills.name: IP Managing
skills.desc: IP managing includes tasks related to managing IP addresses, subnets, and network configurations.

skills.name: URL Data Transferring
skills.desc: URL data transferring involves fetching data from remote URLs and transferring it to local files or processes.

skills.name: Non-interactive Downloading
skills.desc: Non-interactive downloading allows for automated downloads of files from the internet without user interaction.

skills.name: Package Handling
skills.desc: Package handling tasks include installing, updating, and removing software packages on Linux systems.

skills.name: Linux Software
skills.desc: Linux software refers to applications and utilities designed to run on the open-source Linux operating system, offering a wide range of free and customizable tools for various computing tasks.

skills.name: Python Package Installing
skills.desc: Python package installing is specific to managing and installing Python packages using package managers.

skills.name: OpenSSL
skills.desc: OpenSSL is a robust, full-featured toolkit for the Transport Layer Security (TLS) and Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) protocols, and it's also a general-purpose cryptography library.

skills.name: File Comparing
skills.desc: File comparing involves comparing the content of two files to identify differences and similarities.

skills.name: Common Line Comparison
skills.desc: Common line comparison helps identify differences between two text files or directories using common lines.

skills.name: Patch Applying
skills.desc: Applying patches allows for making changes to files based on a patch file, typically used in software development.

skills.name: Text Editing
skills.desc: Text editing involves modifying text files using command-line text editors, enhancing productivity and automation.

skills.name: File Difference Viewing
skills.desc: Viewing file differences provides a way to visualize and understand changes between two text files.

skills.name: Simple Text Editing
skills.desc: Simple text editing covers basic operations like adding, deleting, or modifying text within a file using command-line tools.

skills.name: Graphical Text Editing
skills.desc: Graphical text editing refers to using graphical text editors for more advanced text manipulation tasks.
